methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Positron AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Axelera AI is a Series B-stage semiconductor company founded by Fabrizio Del Maffeo that has raised $200M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of eight private companies and three public semiconductor firms operating at the same or adjacent funding stages, including Positron AI, Etched, d-Matrix, DEEPX, and Substrate among private peers.

Two valuation methods are applicable to this analysis. Stage-progression methodology, anchoring to the $200M total raised as a proxy baseline and applying a typical 1.9x step-up multiplier from Series B forward, implies a valuation of $380M. Peer-median triangulation across six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies yields a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range of $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring using a median P/S multiple of 20.6x across ALAB, NVDA, and AMD cannot be applied, as target revenue remains undisclosed.

The two-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $323M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $690M. This wide band reflects material uncertainty around Axelera's relative competitive positioning and revenue trajectory within a crowded frontier-chip subsector. Stage-progression suggests a more conservative floor, while peer multiples—particularly high-valued Series A peers like Etched at $5.0B—indicate substantial upside scenarios remain plausible for differentiated chip-architecture plays.
